titleStmt.title: Letter from Elizabeth Francis Neill to Lady Clonbrock, 21 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Elizabeth Francis Neill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Elizabeth Francis Neill (b. 1861) to Augusta Caroline Dillon, Lady Clonbrock (1840-1928). Clonbrock was a prominent member of the Irish Women's Association, founded to provide aid and assistance to Irish soldiers and prisoners of war and the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon, 4th Baron Clonbrock. In the letter, Neill thanks Clonbrock for her sympathy over the death of her husband, Richard Herbert Neill (1859-1916). The letter goes on to say that Neill will pass on what Clonbrock has said about the election of a new secretary, stating that the ladies of the tea room will be glad that the position is filled by Charles Joseph Botterill (1862-1959).

msIdentifier.collection: Clonbrock Estate Papers, Collection List No. 54, Ms. 35,796/10/15

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Feb 21 st 1916 Dear Lady Clonbrock I thank you very
much for your kind
expressions of regret
for my dear husband
& sympathy with
us in our great loss. It seems to grow
greater with time.
I shall pass on
what you say about
the election of Secretary
for Tea Room â the
Ladies of the Committee
will doubtless be
very glad to have
the post filled
by Mrs Batterill â
who will I think
always take a
deep interest in
the Temperance
cause here. I remain
your Ladyship's
Most sincerely Elizabeth F Neill
1916
Feb: M rs Neill Headfood Tea
rooms
